title: Cryogenics Engineer — REBCO HTS Magnets
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. Our Gas Dynamic Trap requires a high-field superconducting magnet system using REBCO HTS technology — the same generation of conductor enabling compact-fusion programmes globally.
description: Design, build, and commission the REBCO HTS magnet system for ASPL's GDT. Target central field is in the 8–12 T range using high-temperature superconducting coils in a mirror configuration.
responsibilities: Design REBCO HTS coil geometry for the GDT mirror configuration
Design and specify cryogenic cooling systems (conduction-cooled or cryocooler-based)
Procure REBCO tape and manage coil winding (in-house or vendor)
Commission the magnet system to target field strength
Monitor magnet performance during plasma operations
requirements: M.Tech or PhD in Cryogenics, Superconducting Magnet Engineering, or Applied Physics
Hands-on experience with HTS (REBCO/YBCO) magnet systems
Cryogenic system design in the 4 K–77 K operating range
preferred: Experience with fusion-class magnet systems (ITER, compact-fusion programmes)
REBCO tape characterisation and joint technology
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
